name: manufactured-ask — solved problem dressed as an open blocker (LOW D2)

input.name: James
post: Shipped a since-last-post window for the daybook.

Wins — James shipped the since-last-post window on router-daybook. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed calendar day. He anchored the window to the post's own feed timestamp — the server-provided entry.timestamp returned by the entries endpoint — so it stays correct across machines, with a seven-day fallback when there is no prior post. He also handled the same-day case: a second post on the same day windows from the first post's timestamp, so it only covers new work and never double-counts what the first post already reported.
Insight — Anchoring to the Router's server-provided timestamp rather than the local clock is what makes the window portable across machines, since two machines' clocks cannot be trusted to agree.

Asking — James is looking for a reliable way to anchor the since-last-post window across machines without trusting local clocks. He is asking whether the Router entries endpoint exposes a server-authoritative post timestamp he can read, and at what precision, and for a clean way to window a same-day second post so it only covers new work.

feedEntries.content: Tuning a RAG pipeline for our docs search; chunking strategy is killing recall.
Shipped a Stripe billing portal integration today.

input.workLog: router-daybook: anchored the since-last-post window to the feed's server-provided entry.timestamp (lastOwnPostMs reads it from the entries endpoint); added same-day handling so a second post windows from the first post's timestamp and does not double-count. Both fully implemented and working today.
